summary: Cleaned up and renamed item header types and item callback table fields, added a dedicated item forward-declaration header, updated item code to use the new semantic member names, and added Doxygen-style offset/size annotations. The PR primarily improved `src/melee/it/types.h` by converting anonymous/typedef-heavy struct declarations into named structs with forward declarations, replacing offset-prefixed callback names like `x4_callback_OnSpawn` with intent-based names like `spawned`, and clarifying dynamic bone fields such as `flags`, `skeleton`, and `unk_ptr`. It also adjusted `StageInfo` to use the new `Article` forward type and added `dyn` to the glossary.
what_changed: Added `src/melee/it/forward.h` with forward typedefs for item-related structs including `Article`, `Item`, `ItemAttr`, `ItemLogicTable`, `ItemStateTable`, `SpawnItem`, `itHit`, and several object allocator placeholder structs.
Included `melee/it/forward.h` from both `src/melee/it/types.h` and `src/melee/gr/types.h` so non-item headers can refer to item structs without pulling in the full item type definitions.
Changed `StageInfo` article pointer type from `struct _Article*` to `Article*`.
Reworked item state-change flag macros from raw hex constants and trailing comments into `(1 << n)` definitions with Doxygen comments, including an explicit `ITEM_UNK_0x1` for bit 0.

Renamed `ItemStateTable` fields from offset-style names to semantic names: `x0_state_id` became `asid`, `x4_callback_anim` became `animated`, `x8_callback_phys` became `physics_updated`, and `xC_callback_coll` became `collided`.
Changed `ItemStateContainer` to use `struct ItemStateTable stateTable UNK_SIZE_ARRAY` instead of the old conditional zero-length array form.

Renamed `ItemLogicTable` callback fields from offset/callback names to event-style names such as `states`, `spawned`, `destroyed`, `picked_up`, `dropped`, `thrown`, `dmg_dealt`, `dmg_received`, `entered_air`, `reflected`, `clanked`, `absorbed`, `shield_bounced`, `hit_shield`, and `evt_unk`.
Converted several item structs from `typedef struct ... { ... } Name;` style to named `struct Name { ... };` style, relying on the new forward typedef header for typedef aliases.
Replaced the old `DynamicBones`/`ItemDynamicBones` presentation with an annotated `struct Item_DynamicBones` containing `flags`, `skeleton`, `unk_ptr`, `count`, and `unk_vec`.

Updated `src/melee/it/item.c` and `src/melee/it/item2.c` to use the renamed fields, including callback dispatches through `spawned`, `destroyed`, `picked_up`, `dropped`, `thrown`, `entered_air`, `reflected`, `clanked`, `absorbed`, `shield_bounced`, `hit_shield`, `dmg_dealt`, `dmg_received`, and `evt_unk`.
Added glossary entry `dyn`, defined as short for `dynamic`.
Performed a small formatting cleanup in `func_8026B2B4` by removing unnecessary braces around an early return.
smart_moves: Separated forward declarations into `src/melee/it/forward.h`, reducing cross-header coupling and allowing `gr/types.h` to reference `Article*` without depending on all item type definitions.
Kept source compatibility by pairing named `struct Foo` definitions in `types.h` with typedef forward declarations in `forward.h`, allowing both `struct Foo*` and `Foo*` use where appropriate.
Renamed callback-table fields by event meaning rather than preserving offset-heavy names, making call sites in `item.c` read as lifecycle and collision events instead of raw layout accesses.
Retained explicit layout documentation using `@at{...}` and `@sz{...}` annotations while improving field names, which helps both decomp matching and future documentation.
Used `UNK_SIZE_ARRAY` for an unknown-length state table instead of an `#ifdef M2CTX` zero-length array, centralizing the unknown-size idiom.
Documented uncertain facts with `@todo` rather than overclaiming, e.g. unknown struct sizes and the dynamic-bone flag behavior.
decomp_lessons: When a header becomes a dependency bottleneck, introduce a focused `forward.h` with typedef forward declarations rather than forcing unrelated systems to include the full type header.
Offset-prefixed member names are useful early in decompilation but should be replaced once behavior is understood; keep offsets in Doxygen annotations instead of in the field name.
For callback tables, event-oriented names like `spawned`, `destroyed`, `picked_up`, and `entered_air` make caller code self-documenting and reduce cognitive load during later matching work.
Use `(1 << n)` for bit flags when the source is conceptually a bitmask; it documents the bit index and makes missing or unknown bits explicit.
Unknown or incomplete struct layout should still be represented with named fields, offset annotations, and `@todo` notes so future contributors can refine rather than rediscover the structure.
Forward typedefs can safely bridge systems such as stage and item code when only pointer identity is required.
naming_conventions: Use semantic field names plus Doxygen `@at`/`@sz` layout comments instead of names like `x4_callback_OnSpawn` once the field purpose is known.
Item lifecycle callback names were normalized to past-tense or event names: `spawned`, `destroyed`, `picked_up`, `dropped`, `thrown`, `entered_air`.
Damage and interaction callbacks were named by gameplay event: `dmg_dealt`, `dmg_received`, `reflected`, `clanked`, `absorbed`, `shield_bounced`, `hit_shield`.
Unknown callback names can remain explicitly uncertain with names like `evt_unk` rather than inventing a precise meaning.
Dynamic-bone fields were renamed from layout-only names to conceptual names: `x0_apply_phys_num` to `flags`, `x4_root_jobj` to `skeleton`, and `x8_dynamicBones` to `unk_ptr` at call sites.
Abbreviations should be documented in `docs/glossary.md`; this PR added `dyn` as short for `dynamic`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This PR was mostly header/type cleanup rather than an explicit assembly matching fix; no reviewer evidence describes a specific instruction-level tactic.

Renaming struct members while preserving offsets and sizes supports non-behavioral cleanup: call sites changed names but the underlying layout-sensitive accesses remained aligned with the old offsets.
Using forward declarations avoids include-order and circular-dependency issues without changing binary-relevant code.
Representing unknown-length arrays with `UNK_SIZE_ARRAY` preserves the layout intent needed by decomp tooling while avoiding ad hoc conditional zero-length arrays.

review_feedback.feedback: Suggested moving `@at`/`@sz` annotations to the right side of member declarations because the current per-member Doxygen blocks took up substantial vertical space and made structs harder to scan.
Compared multiple options for struct annotation formatting and argued that right-side annotations were claustrophobic and harder to parse because offsets appear after variable-length declarations.
Noted that the long-term goal is to have at least one sentence, often full paragraphs, per struct member, so source-level struct scanning will become unpleasant regardless of compact formatting.
Raised the option of annotating structs in separate markdown files using Doxygen `@struct` and `@var` style documentation.
Accepted the explanation, saying that once the annotations are fully populated with information the current approach will make more sense.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that build on `src/melee/it/forward.h` or move more item typedefs into forward declarations.
Search for later renames of `Item_DynamicBones` fields `flags`, `skeleton`, `unk_ptr`, `count`, and `unk_vec`.
Check whether later documentation settled on inline Doxygen member comments or separate markdown `@struct`/`@var` documentation.
Search for remaining `x*_callback` names in item headers after PR 677.
Find later PRs that refine `ItemLogicTable::evt_unk` into a more specific callback name.
